title: The Lighthouse Levy
premise: Five coastal towns must jointly fund a lighthouse after a disastrous winter of shipwrecks. Each mayor secretly hopes the others will pay, but storm season is approaching fast. Failure to cooperate means more wrecks and economic ruin.
setting: A coastal region with five independent towns that rely on maritime trade. The central harbor is treacherous without a lighthouse. A recent series of storms has wrecked several ships, and the towns' leaders have gathered in the neutral town of Greyhaven to decide funding.
goal: Secure a fair and sufficient lighthouse funding agreement (at least 100 gold total, with no single town contributing more than 40) before storm season arrives (4 rounds). If agreement is reached, success; if not, wreckage and blame ensue.
gm_notes: Each round, mayors negotiate contributions. Use pressure: storm warnings, economic threats, or accidents. Mayors have private secrets (e.g., some have secretly used the lighthouse site for smuggling). If negotiations stall, introduce a disaster (e.g., a shipwreck with casualties) to force action.

roles.name: Mayor of Greyhaven (Host)
roles.description: Neutral host town; wants a fair deal to avoid blame.
roles.private_info: Greyhaven's harbor is the safest already; your town gains little but you fear being seen as selfish.

roles.name: Mayor of Stonebridge
roles.description: Wealthiest town; trade hub. Can afford the most but resents paying for others.
roles.private_info: Secretly signed a trade deal with a distant kingdom that requires using the dangerous route; a lighthouse would expose the deal.

roles.name: Mayor of Seabrook
roles.description: Fishing town; poor but hardest hit by wrecks.
roles.private_info: You've secretly been salvaging wrecks for extra income; a lighthouse would end that practice.

roles.name: Mayor of Windmere
roles.description: Agricultural town; little use for lighthouse but fears higher taxes if others underpay.
roles.private_info: You have a hidden stash of gold from illegal timber trade; could secretly fund the lighthouse but don't want to reveal it.

npcs.name: Elder Harlow
npcs.description: Respected town elder of Greyhaven, neutral mediator.
npcs.hidden_agenda: Wants a quick deal to avoid conflict; secretly owns the only shipyard that can build the lighthouse.

npcs.name: Captain Voss
npcs.description: Grizzled sea captain, witness to the wrecks.
npcs.hidden_agenda: Lost his brother in a wreck; will sabotage any deal that doesn't include a lighthouse this season.

npcs.name: Merchant Liana
npcs.description: Wealthy trader from Stonebridge, loud advocate for lighthouse.
npcs.hidden_agenda: Her ships are insured; she profits from wreck salvage and actually wants delays to collect insurance.

npcs.name: Fisherman Kale
npcs.description: Young fisherman from Seabrook, desperate for safety.
npcs.hidden_agenda: Plans to steal from any collected fund to pay off his debts; will try to manipulate the deal.
